consensus: Proven max-propulsion marathon shoe; bouncy but bulky next to newer rivals.
athleteNotes: Kiptum's official 2:00:35 world-record shoe; Kiplimo won Chicago 2025 in it.

consensus: A return to aggression — smoother and wider-riding than the Alphafly 3.
athleteNotes: Kiplimo ran 2:00:28 at London 2026 in it.

consensus: Lightest mainline Vaporfly ever; snappier lower-stack ride — the consensus default racer.
athleteNotes: The most common amateur PB shoe of the supershoe era.

consensus: Light and fast with arguably the best upper in a race shoe; softer than the Pro 3.
The first sub-2:00 shoe — astonishingly light but fragile and built for one or two races.
athleteNotes: Sawe's 1:59:30 and Assefa's 2:15:41 world records at London 2026.

consensus: Road Trail Run's 2025 marathon-race winner — one of the very best racers, suited to stride-style runners.
athleteNotes: Asics Metaspeed line dominated Boston 2026 (7 of top 10 in both fields).

consensus: Near-identical praise to the Sky — pick by running style (cadence over stride).
Believe in the Run's race shoe of 2025 — the lightest legal supershoe; not one for heel strikers and durability is a known trade.
athleteNotes: On the women's podium at Boston 2026.

consensus: The softest, bounciest racer on the market — 5K speed to marathon versatility, with stability the trade-off.
Great-value supershoe with a friendlier ride than the Elite.
The lightest, fastest Hoka racer to date — a hugely bouncy rockered ride.
The stable, more traditional alternative to the Cielo X1.
Sharper than v4 and more of a true racer; the strong wide-foot race pick.
Puma's efficiency-study shoe — claimed >3% economy gain over rivals; Doctors of Running's favourite long-distance racer of 2025.
As fast as anything on the market with a more natural feel.
Fun and fast; the LightSpray version carried Obiri to the NYC 2025 course record.
athleteNotes: Obiri won NYC 2025 (2:19:51 CR) in the LightSpray edition.

consensus: Criminally overlooked — a natural-feeling all-distance racer.
Mizuno's new flagship racing line — as fast as anything for shorter races.
Polarising but credible: firm, aggressive and very low drop — best for midfoot strikers.
Believe in the Run's best short-distance speed shoe of 2025 — the purest speed rush for 5K and 10K.
Stripped-down 184g racer with two Lightstrike Pro layers and Energyrods; the lineup's lightest weapon for 5K-10K.
Decathlon's marathon racer: PEBA VFOAM Plus with full carbon plate at £180 — the same physics as £250 supershoes, less the brand premium.
The tempo GOAT — RunRepeat's best tempo shoe of 2026, versatile enough to race.
The best-value plated trainer — a daily-to-tempo workhorse with outstanding outsole durability.
Light, snappy entry-level carbon trainer — the affordable gateway to plated speed.
Road Trail Run's plated max-trainer of 2025 — Hoka's best all-rounder.
The March 2026 update of the super-trainer king — softer, lighter, the best Superblast yet.
Road Trail Run's unplated-max winner of 2025 — intervals-to-easy versatility in one shoe.
RunRepeat's best shock absorption — insanely fun and super-plush.
Road Trail Run's Road Shoe of the Year 2025 — light, bouncy and cheap.
Capable but divisive — the high-set plate rides harsh next to the best tempo shoes.
A plated daily that does everything well — strong value.
Skechers' tempo trainer — moderate stack, carbon-infused H-plate and Goodyear outsole; nimble at 242 g.
The Hoka super-trainer reference: lightweight ProFly+ ride for daily-to-tempo work without a plate.
On's lightweight tempo trainer reborn with Helion Hyper; firm and direct with a Speedboard plate underfoot.
Super-trainer with an exaggerated heel bevel and forward-tilted rocker; excellent shock absorption for the price.
On's first true super-trainer: max-stack with Helion HF PEBA and a Speedboard; firm, durable, heavy, but stable.
The consensus shoe of 2025-26 — supershoe foam at a trainer price; best overall for BITR, RunRepeat and the Reddit crowd alike.
RunRepeat's best daily trainer of 2026 — the benchmark fun-per-pound trainer.
The biggest Pegasus update in years — claimed 15% more energy return.
The safe default daily — widths from narrow to 4E.
Keeps the balanced formula that made the Ride 18 Doctors of Running's top daily of 2025.
Believe in the Run's best daily trainer of 2025 — the best version yet and standout value.
The classic daily trainer, totally modernised.
On's best pure daily — soft, smooth and rockered.
Higher stack and a new 8 mm drop — the dependable easy-day staple.
A max-stack cruiser — the cheaper 1080 alternative.
Altra's gateway low-drop trainer with the signature foot-shaped toe box.
Cushioned with an anatomical toe box — the Altra alternative that keeps some drop.
Real max-stack daily with rigid CloudTec ride; heavy and firm but stable and durable.
PEBA-injected take on the Cloudmonster: notably lighter and bouncier with a Speedboard nudging it toward super-trainer territory.
Updated PEBA-blend Dreamstrike+ midsole makes the Rise 3 a versatile, well-priced daily trainer for easy and tempo miles.
Pillowy DNA Tuned ride with the wider-than-ever Glycerin 23 platform; the consensus plush daily.
Between-the-lines Clifton/Bondi cruiser with a smooth rocker and forgiving easy-day feel.
Lighter Cumulus with more FF Blast Plus Eco underfoot; the safe, versatile daily that gets out of the way.
Underrated workhorse daily with HOVR+ supercritical foam — durable, comfortable, no frills.
All-new A-TPU IncrediLUX foam drops weight ~60g and adds bounce; one of the biggest year-on-year jumps in the line.
Trail-DNA road daily with eTPU optiFOAM; surprisingly light and grippy, very Salomon.
Brooks' lightweight value daily; nitrogen-infused DNA Flash v2 keeps the ride lively under £100.
Long-distance daily with carbon-infused H-plate and Hyper Burst Ice foam; APMA-accepted and surprisingly capable for the price.
Bouncy Lightstrike-class Floatzig foam reborn under Reebok; surprising daily at the price.
Huge value max-cushion daily with wide options.
Believe in the Run's best do-it-all of 2025 and a Doctors of Running favourite recovery shoe.
Nike's biggest-ever stack — best max-cushion of 2025 for BITR and RTR's easy/recovery pick.
The lightest Nimbus ever — plush, though some call it dated at the price.
The plushest 1080 to date — RunRepeat's daily-cushion pick for 2026.
The default max-cushion walk/run shoe — softer than ever.
The most cushioned Hoka — plated bounce for easy and long days.
PEBA softness in a durable daily package.
Brooks' rolling max-stack cruiser.
On's max-cushion long-run shoe, succeeding the Cloudeclipse.
The sleeper max-cushion value pick — under the radar at BITR.
Maximalist low-drop cruiser with deep, plush Fresh Foam X; available in 2E for the wider-foot crowd.
Wide-platform max-cushion daily; stable, durable and forgiving without the high-stack instability.
Mizuno's max-stack plush daily; Enerzy NXT layered over standard Enerzy for soft-but-stable cruising.
Premium-tier daily with a triple-stack midsole (Air Zoom + ZoomX + ReactX) for the most cushioned Pegasus ever.
Puma's max-stack daily — high stack with Nitro Elite foam keeping the weight reasonable for its size.
RunRepeat's best stability shoe of 2026 — stable as a table with best-in-class grip.
Lighter and more versatile than the Kayano — the best overall stability pick for many testers.
Believe in the Run's best stability shoe of 2025 — Nike's stability comeback.
The modern broad-base guidance approach — support without posts, at strong value.
The Run Testers' best stability shoe of 2026 — the lightest, softest stability shoe around.
Premium guidance for moderate-to-severe overpronators.
Top-tier cushioned stability — pricey but plush.
Modern-geometry stability praised across BITR and Doctors of Running.
The classic mechanical-support holdout, modernised.
Subtle-stability super-trainer: PWRRUN PB core with a firmer PWRRUN frame guiding the foot; lively without feeling corrective.
Premium guidance daily: Enerzy NXT plush layer over firmer Enerzy base, broad platform, very heavy but very stable.
Heavier, wider-than-ever guidance shoe using NB's wedge-density Fresh Foam X with an integrated stability plate.
Dreamstrike+ PEBA-blend midsole with refined medial post and higher sidewalls — the most modern-feeling Adidas support shoe yet.
HOKA's premium stability cruiser: ultra-wide platform, roomy fit, but the CMEVA midsole feels dated next to peers.
RunRepeat's best cheap shoe — the best cushioning-weight-price mix in the bracket.
Luxurious for the price — the best budget shock absorption per RunRepeat.
The most durable outsole in the budget bracket — a firm, stable daily.
The best cushion-per-pound max-stack budget shoe.
Marshmallowy recovery and long-run value — a RunRepeat pick.
Lightweight budget daily with the Trace's signature smooth-and-soft feel and a £90 RRP.
Budget value-pick: refreshed DNA Loft v2 midsole and tidied outsole make it strong for beginners and casual runners.
The series stays low-stack low-drop while the rest of the market goes max — the closest thing to an old-school neutral lightweight left.
Budget hero: plush FuelCell midsole at £100-ish; the punch-above-its-weight value pick for easy-pace runners.
Decathlon's lightweight £100 daily; VFOAM midsole offers surprising bounce in a sub-225g shoe.
